translation.description: Sophia derives from the Greek word 'sophia' meaning wisdom. It has been one of the most popular baby names globally for over a decade.
translation.culturalNotes: The name has deep roots in Greek philosophy and early Christianity. The Hagia Sophia in Istanbul was named after divine wisdom.
translation.metaDescription: Sophia means wisdom in Greek. One of the most popular girl names worldwide. Discover its origin, famous bearers, and cultural significance.

translation.description: Liam is an Irish short form of William, derived from the Germanic elements 'wil' (will, desire) and 'helm' (helmet, protection). It has topped baby name charts in the US since 2017.
translation.culturalNotes: Originally an Irish diminutive of Uilliam (the Irish form of William), Liam became a standalone name and spread globally from Ireland.
translation.metaDescription: Liam means strong-willed warrior. An Irish form of William, it has been the top boy name in the US. Learn its Celtic and Germanic roots.

translation.description: Olivia derives from the Latin word 'oliva' meaning olive tree. Shakespeare is credited with first using the name in his play Twelfth Night (1602).
translation.culturalNotes: The olive tree has been a symbol of peace, wisdom, and prosperity since ancient times. Shakespeare popularized Olivia as a given name in English-speaking countries.
translation.metaDescription: Olivia means olive tree, a symbol of peace. First used by Shakespeare, it remains one of the top girl names. Explore its Latin origin.

translation.description: Noah comes from the Hebrew name Noach, meaning rest or comfort. In the Bible, Noah built the ark that saved his family and pairs of animals from the Great Flood.
translation.culturalNotes: Noah is a significant figure in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. The story of Noah's Ark is one of the most widely known biblical narratives across cultures.
translation.metaDescription: Noah means rest and comfort in Hebrew. A timeless biblical name among the top boy names for years. Discover its meaning and significance.

translation.description: Emma originates from the Germanic word 'ermen' meaning whole or universal. It was borne by several medieval queens and popularized by Jane Austen's novel Emma (1815).
translation.culturalNotes: Emma was the name of several European queens in the Middle Ages. Jane Austen's 1815 novel brought the name into literary prominence in the English-speaking world.
translation.metaDescription: Emma means whole or universal in Germanic. A classic name loved for centuries, from medieval queens to Jane Austen. Explore its history.

translation.description: James is the English form of the Late Latin name Iacomus, derived from the Hebrew name Yaakov (Jacob), meaning supplanter. It has been used by six US presidents and numerous kings.
translation.culturalNotes: James has been one of the most enduring names in the English-speaking world. Six US presidents have borne the name, and it remains a symbol of classic strength.
translation.metaDescription: James means supplanter, from the Hebrew Jacob. A timeless classic borne by kings and presidents. Learn about this enduring boy name.

translation.description: Charlotte is the French feminine diminutive of Charles, which derives from the Germanic 'karl' meaning free man. It has been a royal favorite for centuries across Europe.
translation.culturalNotes: The name gained renewed global attention with the birth of Princess Charlotte of Cambridge in 2015. Charlotte Bronte brought literary fame to the name in the 19th century.
translation.metaDescription: Charlotte means free woman, the French feminine form of Charles. A royal and literary classic. Discover its Germanic roots and history.

translation.description: Oliver likely derives from the Latin 'olivarius' (olive tree planter) or the Old French name Olivier. It was popularized by the medieval French epic La Chanson de Roland.
translation.culturalNotes: The name was widely known through Charles Dickens' novel Oliver Twist (1837). It also appears in the medieval legend of Roland, where Olivier is Roland's closest companion.
translation.metaDescription: Oliver means olive tree, from Latin and French origins. Made iconic by Dickens and medieval legend. A top boy name worldwide.

translation.description: Amelia derives from the Germanic name Amalia, rooted in the element 'amal' meaning work or industriousness. It became associated with bravery through aviator Amelia Earhart.
translation.culturalNotes: Amelia Earhart, the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic, cemented this name as a symbol of courage and determination. It was also common among European royalty.
translation.metaDescription: Amelia means industrious and hardworking from Germanic roots. Made iconic by aviator Amelia Earhart. A beloved and timeless girl name.

translation.description: Elijah comes from the Hebrew name Eliyahu, composed of 'el' (God) and 'Yah' (Yahweh). In the Bible, Elijah was one of the greatest and most powerful prophets.
translation.culturalNotes: Elijah is a major prophet in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. In Jewish tradition, a seat is reserved for Elijah at every Passover seder and circumcision ceremony.
translation.metaDescription: Elijah means my God is Yahweh in Hebrew. A powerful biblical prophet's name rising in popularity. Explore its deep spiritual roots.

translation.description: Ava may derive from the Latin 'avis' (bird) or the Hebrew name Chava (Eve), meaning life or living one. Its modern popularity is often attributed to actress Ava Gardner.
translation.culturalNotes: The name gained Hollywood glamour through Ava Gardner in the 1940s and 1950s. It may also be connected to a medieval Germanic element meaning desired.
translation.metaDescription: Ava means life or birdlike, with Latin and Hebrew roots. Made glamorous by Ava Gardner. A short, elegant, and top-ranking girl name.

translation.description: William comes from the Germanic name Willahelm, composed of 'wil' (will, desire) and 'helm' (helmet, protection). It was introduced to England by William the Conqueror in 1066.
translation.culturalNotes: William has been borne by numerous kings of England and remains one of the most enduring names in the English language, used consistently for nearly a thousand years.
translation.metaDescription: William means resolute protector from Germanic roots. Borne by kings and Shakespeare alike. One of the most enduring names in English.

translation.description: Luna comes directly from the Latin word for moon. In Roman mythology, Luna was the divine personification of the moon, equivalent to the Greek goddess Selene.
translation.culturalNotes: Luna was the Roman goddess who drove her moon chariot across the night sky. The name has surged in popularity partly due to the character Luna Lovegood in Harry Potter.
translation.metaDescription: Luna means moon in Latin. Named after the Roman moon goddess. A celestial name that has soared in popularity. Discover its origin.

translation.meaning: Son of the right hand, son of the south
translation.description: Benjamin derives from the Hebrew name Binyamin, meaning son of the right hand. In the Bible, Benjamin was the youngest son of Jacob and Rachel.
translation.culturalNotes: In biblical tradition, the right hand symbolizes strength and favor. Benjamin was the founder of one of the twelve tribes of Israel, making it a name of deep significance.
translation.metaDescription: Benjamin means son of the right hand in Hebrew. A strong biblical name carried by Benjamin Franklin. Explore its enduring appeal.

translation.meaning: Devoted to God, God is my oath
translation.description: Isabella is an Italian and Spanish form of Elizabeth, ultimately from the Hebrew name Elisheva, meaning God is my oath. It was borne by many medieval queens of Europe.
translation.culturalNotes: Queen Isabella I of Castile funded Christopher Columbus's voyage to the Americas in 1492. The name has been a royal favorite across Spain, Italy, and England for centuries.
translation.metaDescription: Isabella means devoted to God, from Hebrew via Italian. A royal name borne by queens across Europe. Discover its rich history.
